notes: Harder baseline after adding spec completion, phase completion, asset manifest, visual geometry, and startup replay gates.
Mechanical producers added for scroll QA visual geometry and delegate run startup/phase summaries; current warehouse produced geometry fails blank-band ratio.
Artifact summary now rejects placeholder handoffs; candidate-output provenance tests regenerate phase/startup/spec/asset summaries from declared artifact-shaped sources.
Added a bounded phase prompt compiler, startup probe command builder, and producer-backed compiled-prompt eval row before another live Pi/Kimi run.
Live startup probe passed with --thinking low and a 90s first-write gate; sanitized success fixture added to replay suite.
Headline metric now scores expected accept/reject outcomes; raw assertion pass rate remains diagnostic. Startup probe paths, short-output completion, and handoff section evidence were hardened.
Live compiled spec produced first-write, complete SPEC.md, and non-placeholder handoff, but exit 124 keeps phase completion rejected; process timeout is now a durable reject-control case.
Review blockers fixed: prose-only stubs no longer parse fake route IDs, and provenance tests derive output markers from declared sources before checking must_contain rules.
Added opt-in --complete-when-output-and-handoff to stop bounded phase runs after expected output plus completed handoff; public prompt eval now requires the flag.
Live compiled spec retry wrote a complete 16 KB SPEC.md, produced first-write proof and a non-placeholder handoff, exited 0, and is now accepted as a positive fixture.

Live asset phase generated real media and a lint-clean manifest, but wrapper exit 124 keeps the phase rejected; heading detection now accepts the live handoff variants while the profile asks for canonical headings.
No-spend Pi/Kimi asset finalization wrote readiness proof, ran the manifest linter, produced canonical handoff sections, and completed through expected_output_and_handoff.

Fixed review blockers: unsafe out-of-root asset refs are rejected, handoff completion requires non-empty required bodies, wrapper clean completion requires the expected output in changed files, and unsafe-ref fixture is an expected reject.
